{"id":"workspace-svs","title":"[security] Audit event FK ordering in provisioning D1 batch","description":"File: specs/012-clawtask-vertical-slice/plan.md (Security Considerations)
Severity: Critical

Problem:
Audit events in the D1 batch must be inserted last — foreign keys on workspace_id, actor_id, etc. must exist before audit events reference them, or the batch will fail with FK constraint errors.

Fix:
Order D1 batch statements: 1) workspace, 2) actor, 3) areas+contexts, 4) audit events. Verify FK ordering in WorkspaceProvisioningService.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","owner":"david@worldsenoughstudios.com","created_at":"2026-03-03T17:14:57Z","created_by":"eykd","updated_at":"2026-03-03T17:14:57Z"}

{"id":"workspace-35c","title":"[security] Empty actor ID in requireWorkspace must 503, not fall through","description":"File: specs/012-clawtask-vertical-slice/plan.md (Security Considerations)
Severity: Critical

Problem:
requireWorkspace middleware must return 503 when actor ID is empty — falling through with empty string produces silent data corruption (audit events with empty actor_id, actions owned by nobody).

Fix:
Add explicit check: if (!actorId) return c.json({ error: 'Workspace not provisioned' }, 503);","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","owner":"david@worldsenoughstudios.com","created_at":"2026-03-03T17:14:54Z","created_by":"eykd","updated_at":"2026-03-03T17:14:54Z"}

new_value: Implemented requireWorkspace middleware with explicit actor null check returning 503. Created WorkspaceRepository and ActorRepository interfaces, updated AppEnv.Variables with workspaceId and actorId, and added 9 tests covering all security paths.

{"description":"**Spec**: specs/012-clawtask-vertical-slice/spec.md §US01
**Goal**: When a new user signs up, their workspace, actor identity, and default seed data (3 Areas + 5 Contexts) are automatically created before the user ever reaches the application.
**Acceptance**: Register a new account, immediately hit any authenticated endpoint, verify workspace + actor + areas + contexts all exist. GET /api/v1/areas returns 3 seeded areas. GET /api/v1/contexts returns 5 seeded contexts.

---
## Implementation Constraints (merged from remediation tasks)

**[Critical] requireWorkspace: empty actorId must return 503, not fall through** (workspace-35c)
Add explicit null guard: if (!actorId) return c.json({ error: 'Workspace not provisioned' }, 503); — falling through with empty string produces silent data corruption (audit events with empty actor_id, actions owned by nobody).

**[Critical] D1 batch FK ordering: audit events must be inserted last** (workspace-svs)
Order D1 batch statements: 1) workspace, 2) actor, 3) areas+contexts, 4) audit events. Audit events have FKs on workspace_id and actor_id which must exist first or the batch fails with FK constraint errors.

**[High] All D1 statements must use .bind() — no string interpolation** (workspace-40o)
WorkspaceProvisioningService must use parameterized statements (.bind(...params)) for all external values. Direct string interpolation in SQL is a SQL injection vulnerability.

**[High] Provisioning audit events: actor_id must be the newly created human actor ID** (workspace-85o)
Use the newly created human actor's ID for all provisioning audit events (workspace, areas, contexts). Do not leave actor_id empty or use a synthetic system actor.

**[High] WorkspaceProvisioningService must be a thin D1-batch adapter called by ProvisionWorkspaceUseCase** (workspace-v4b)
Do not put domain orchestration in WorkspaceProvisioningService. The auth hook calls ProvisionWorkspaceUseCase; the use case calls the service as a D1-batch transport. Business logic stays in the application layer."}

GET /api/v1/areas returns 3 seeded areas. GET /api/v1/contexts returns 5 seeded contexts.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"david@worldsenoughstudios.com","created_at":"2026-03-03T18:20:05Z","created_by":"eykd","updated_at":"2026-03-03T18:20:05Z"}

{"description":"**Spec**: specs/012-clawtask-vertical-slice/spec.md §US03
**Goal**: A user can instantly capture a thought/task/commitment to their inbox without upfront categorisation. POST /api/v1/inbox creates inbox item. GET /api/v1/inbox lists all workspace inbox items.
**Acceptance**: POST with title → 201 + inbox item with status=inbox + audit event. POST with empty title → 400. GET → list of workspace inbox items.

---
## Implementation Constraints (merged from remediation tasks)

**[High] InboxItemRepository interface must accept optional status filter** (workspace-2bk)
Define listByWorkspaceId(workspaceId: string, status?: InboxItemStatus): Promise<InboxItem[]> in the domain interface. Without the optional param the concrete D1 class will not satisfy the interface and TypeScript will error.

**[Critical] GET /api/v1/inbox filters to status='inbox' only — document this explicitly** (workspace-7tc)
The plan silently filters to status='inbox' only, but the spec says 'list of workspace inbox items' with no filter. Explicitly state in the acceptance criteria that GET /api/v1/inbox returns only status='inbox' items (clarified items are not shown). Accepted decision: filter to inbox status only, matching the plan.

**[Medium] Validate Content-Type: application/json before calling req.json()** (workspace-89u)
Add Content-Type header validation (or a Hono middleware) before all req.json() calls in InboxApiHandlers to avoid parse errors and unexpected behavior with malformed requests."}

POST /api/v1/inbox creates inbox item. GET /api/v1/inbox lists all workspace inbox items.\n**Acceptance**: POST with title → 201 + inbox item with status=inbox + audit event. POST with empty title → 400. GET → list of workspace inbox items.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"david@worldsenoughstudios.com","created_at":"2026-03-03T18:20:13Z","created_by":"eykd","updated_at":"2026-03-03T18:20:13Z"}

new_value:
{"description":"**Spec**: specs/012-clawtask-vertical-slice/spec.md §US04
**Goal**: A user can process an inbox item and turn it into a concrete, actionable task by assigning it an Area and Context. Atomic D1 batch: inbox item → clarified, new action → ready.
**Acceptance**: POST /api/v1/inbox/:id/clarify with areaId+contextId → inbox item status=clarified + new action status=ready + 2 audit events. Already-clarified item → 422. Invalid area → 422. Invalid context → 422.

---
## Implementation Constraints (merged from remediation tasks)

**[Critical] ClarifyInboxItemToAction: use optimistic lock via WHERE status='inbox'** (workspace-gib)
Implement atomic conditional UPDATE: UPDATE inbox_item SET status='clarified' WHERE id=? AND status='inbox'; check rows affected = 1 before proceeding with action creation. Without this, concurrent clarify requests cause a TOCTOU race producing double-clarification.

**[High] Clarify request: description is an optional override, not required** (workspace-g0u)
The action inherits the inbox item's description automatically. The POST /api/v1/inbox/:id/clarify request body may accept an optional description field to override the inherited value. Update FR-002 and openapi.yaml to make this explicit.

**[High] Define ClarifyInboxItemResultDto { inboxItem, action } in data-model.md** (workspace-40y)
Add ClarifyInboxItemResultDto to the DTOs section of data-model.md matching the { inboxItem, action } shape already in openapi.yaml. The response shape must not be invented ad hoc at implementation time.

**[High] Migration: add clarified_into_id FK with REFERENCES action(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT** (workspace-089)
The clarified_into_id column on inbox_item table must have REFERENCES action(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT to prevent orphaned clarification references. Include this constraint in the D1 migration for the inbox_item table."}
old_value: {"id":"workspace-bms.1.2","title":"US04: Clarify Inbox Item into Standalone Action","description":"**Spec**: specs/012-clawtask-vertical-slice/spec.md §US04\n**Goal**: A user can process an inbox item and turn it into a concrete, actionable task by assigning it an Area and Context. Atomic D1 batch: inbox item → clarified, new action → ready.\n**Acceptance**: POST /api/v1/inbox/:id/clarify with areaId+contextId → inbox item status=clarified + new action status=ready + 2 audit events. Already-clarified item → 422. Invalid area → 422. Invalid context → 422.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"david@worldsenoughstudios.com","created_at":"2026-03-03T18:19:51Z","created_by":"eykd","updated_at":"2026-03-03T18:19:51Z"}

new_value:
{"description":"**Spec**: specs/012-clawtask-vertical-slice/spec.md §US05
**Goal**: A user can move an action through its lifecycle: ready → active → done. Each transition recorded as audit event. GET /api/v1/actions lists actions (optional ?status= filter).
**Acceptance**: POST activate on ready action → active + audit event. POST complete on active → done + audit event. POST activate on done → 422. POST complete on ready → 422.

---
## Implementation Constraints (merged from remediation tasks)

**[Critical] No raw D1 SQL in use cases — state transitions via ActionRepository methods** (workspace-uku)
ActivateActionUseCase and CompleteActionUseCase must NOT call db.prepare()/stmt.run() directly. The application layer must never import infrastructure. Add activateById and completeById to ActionRepository in src/domain/interfaces/ and implement the conditional UPDATE in D1ActionRepository.

**[High] ActionRepository interface: add activateById and completeById returning Promise<boolean>** (workspace-yar)
Add activateById(id: string, workspaceId: string): Promise<boolean> and completeById(id: string, workspaceId: string): Promise<boolean> to the ActionRepository interface in src/domain/interfaces/ActionRepository.ts. Return true if the row was updated, false if not found or wrong state (enables 422 detection in use case).

**[High] Validate ?status= query param against enum before SQL — return 400 for invalid values** (workspace-v6l)
The ?status= parameter in GET /api/v1/actions must be validated against ['ready','active','done'] before use in D1 query. Absent param = return all statuses. Empty string or invalid value = 400. Do not pass unvalidated strings into SQL."}
old_value: {"id":"workspace-bms.1.5","title":"US05: Activate and Complete a Standalone Action","description":"**Spec**: specs/012-clawtask-vertical-slice/spec.md §US05\n**Goal**: A user can move an action through its lifecycle: ready → active → done. Each transition recorded as audit event. GET /api/v1/actions lists actions (optional ?status= filter).\n**Acceptance**: POST activate on ready action → active + audit event. POST complete on active → done + audit event. POST activate on done → 422. POST complete on ready → 422.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"david@worldsenoughstudios.com","created_at":"2026-03-03T18:20:19Z","created_by":"eykd","updated_at":"2026-03-03T18:20:19Z"}

new_value:
{"description":"**Spec**: specs/012-clawtask-vertical-slice/spec.md §US02
**Goal**: Users can perform the full inbox→clarify→activate→complete flow through a web interface without API calls. Three pages: Dashboard (/app), Inbox (/app/inbox), Actions (/app/actions).
**Acceptance**: Dashboard shows inbox count + active count + Quick Capture form. Capture submits without full reload. Clarify shows inline form with area/context selects. Actions page with Activate/Complete buttons updating inline. Full flow without page reload.

---
## Implementation Constraints (merged from remediation tasks)

**[High] CSP blocks hx-on::after-settle — use Alpine.js x-on:htmx:after-settle.window** (workspace-1ea)
Do not use hx-on::after-settle= attributes in any template. Content Security Policy blocks inline event handlers. Use Alpine.js x-on:htmx:after-settle.window= equivalents instead.

**[High] All HTMX mutation handlers must validate _csrf against __Host-csrf cookie** (workspace-kzv)
Implement CSRF middleware that reads the __Host-csrf cookie and compares it to the _csrf form field. Apply to all state-changing Hono routes (POST, PUT, DELETE). Missing validation enables CSRF attacks.

**[High] All templates must use html tagged template literal — no raw string concatenation** (workspace-mwz)
All HTML templates in src/presentation/templates/ must use html`...` (or the project's escaping utility) for any user-supplied values. Plain string interpolation into HTML is XSS-vulnerable.

**[High] User-specific HTML responses must set Cache-Control: no-store** (workspace-cpy)
All authenticated page responses (inbox, actions, workspace pages) must include Cache-Control: no-store header to prevent sensitive user data from being cached by CDN or browser."}

Three pages: Dashboard (/app), Inbox (/app/inbox), Actions (/app/actions).\n**Acceptance**: Dashboard shows inbox count + active count + Quick Capture form. Capture submits without full reload. Clarify shows inline form with area/context selects. Actions page with Activate/Complete buttons updating inline. Full flow without page reload.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"david@worldsenoughstudios.com","created_at":"2026-03-03T18:19:51Z","created_by":"eykd","updated_at":"2026-03-03T18:19:51Z"}

old_value:
{"id":"workspace-bms.1.3","title":"US01: Workspace Auto-Provisioning on Signup","description":"**Spec**: specs/012-clawtask-vertical-slice/spec.md §US01
**Goal**: When a new user signs up, their workspace, actor identity, and default seed data (3 Areas + 5 Contexts) are automatically created before the user ever reaches the application.
**Acceptance**: Register a new account, immediately hit any authenticated endpoint, verify workspace + actor + areas + contexts all exist. GET /api/v1/areas returns 3 seeded areas. GET /api/v1/contexts returns 5 seeded contexts.

---
## Implementation Constraints (merged from remediation tasks)

**[Critical] requireWorkspace: empty actorId must return 503, not fall through** (workspace-35c)
Add explicit null guard: if (!actorId) return c.json({ error: 'Workspace not provisioned' }, 503); — falling through with empty string produces silent data corruption (audit events with empty actor_id, actions owned by nobody).

**[Critical] D1 batch FK ordering: audit events must be inserted last** (workspace-svs)
Order D1 batch statements: 1) workspace, 2) actor, 3) areas+contexts, 4) audit events. Audit events have FKs on workspace_id and actor_id which must exist first or the batch fails with FK constraint errors.

**[High] All D1 statements must use .bind() — no string interpolation** (workspace-40o)
WorkspaceProvisioningService must use parameterized statements (.bind(...params)) for all external values. Direct string interpolation in SQL is a SQL injection vulnerability.

**[High] Provisioning audit events: actor_id must be the newly created human actor ID** (workspace-85o)
Use the newly created human actor's ID for all provisioning audit events (workspace, areas, contexts). Do not leave actor_id empty or use a synthetic system actor.

**[High] WorkspaceProvisioningService must be a thin D1-batch adapter called by ProvisionWorkspaceUseCase** (workspace-v4b)
Do not put domain orchestration in WorkspaceProvisioningService. The auth hook calls ProvisionWorkspaceUseCase; the use case calls the service as a D1-batch transport. Business logic stays in the application layer.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"david@worldsenoughstudios.com","created_at":"2026-03-03T18:20:05Z","created_by":"eykd","updated_at":"2026-03-03T19:49:14Z"}
